ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for filled donut; SNOMED CT coverage of specific bakery snack subtypes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard filled donut weighs approximately 75 g and represents a typical single-serve snack portion.

nutrient_source.title: Filled Donut — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for filled donut (cr_id F11100004), confidence 0.95. All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are preserved verbatim. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling' (FDC ID 172757) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; the USDA value of approximately 8.5 g/100 g is used as an analogous estimate and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for filled donuts in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 76 is taken from the closest analogous entry: 'Doughnut, cake type' (GI=76, glucose reference) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Filled donuts share the same deep-fried refined-flour dough base; the filling (cream, custard, jam) adds sugar but does not materially lower the GI of the dough matrix. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (76) × available carbohydrate per serving (41.33 g/100 g × 75 g serving = 31.0 g) / 100 = 23.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (19.84 g/100 g), high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), and moderate sodium (234 mg/100 g) with negligible potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid filled donuts; the combination of high sugar, high saturated fat, and moderate sodium promotes arterial stiffness and fluid retention, and a single 75 g serving delivers approximately 175 mg sodium with no meaningful potassium or fibre buffer.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (19.84 g/100 g) and high glycemic index (76) with low dietary fibre (2.19 g/100 g) causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid filled donuts; a single 75 g serving delivers approximately 15 g of sugar and has a glycemic load of 23.6, causing a sharp postprandial glucose spike that is poorly buffered by the low fibre content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), likely trans fat from deep-frying in shortening, high refined sugar, and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid filled donuts; the high saturated fat, probable trans fat from hydrogenated shortening used in deep-frying, and high refined sugar content collectively raise LDL cholesterol and promote systemic inflammation, increasing cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and likely trans fat; high refined sugar (19.84 g/100 g) and high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g) are strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid filled donuts entirely; the combination of AGEs from deep-frying, probable trans fat, high saturated fat, and high refined sugar directly exacerbates joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A filled donut is a deep-fried sweet dough product made from enriched refined wheat flour, sugar, eggs, milk, yeast, and vegetable shortening or palm oil, injected or filled with cream, custard, jam, or chocolate after frying. The exterior is soft and lightly golden with a slightly crisp surface from deep-frying; the interior is pillowy and yielding, with a rich, sweet filling that varies by flavour. Filled donuts are classified as ultra-processed snack foods and are widely available in bakeries, convenience stores, and fast-food chains across Singapore and internationally. Per 100 g, a filled donut provides 412 kcal, 7.01 g protein, 24.35 g fat (approximately 8.5 g saturated), 41.33 g carbohydrate, 19.84 g sugar, 2.19 g dietary fibre, and 234 mg sodium. The glycemic index is high (approximately 76), and a standard 75 g serving carries a glycemic load of approximately 23.6. Clinically, filled donuts are unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to their very high sugar content, high saturated fat, probable trans fat from deep-frying in shortening, and low fibre. The deep-frying process also generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory. Filled donuts should be treated as an occasional indulgence and avoided by those with cardiometabolic or inflammatory conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a filled donut?
Can a diabetic eat a filled donut?
Is a filled donut high in sugar?
What is the GI of a donut?
How much fat is in a cream-filled donut?
Is a jelly donut bad for blood pressure?
Can I eat a donut if I have heart disease?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100004, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from the closest USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (FDC ID 172757, yeast-leavened doughnut with creme filling) and documented. GI estimated from the closest University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry (cake-type doughnut, GI=76) per Atkinson et al. 2008 and documented. All adaptations are transparent.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (8.5 g/100 g) is an analogous estimate from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 172757 ('Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling'); HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. 2. The following fields are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific filled donut formulation are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for cr_id F11100004, and the matched USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (FDC ID 172757) does not provide complete values for all of these fields as of 2026-04-16; limited published data exist for the specific commercial preparation method used: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FDC ID 172757 for this formulation); polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FDC ID 172757 for this formulation); trans_fat_g set to -1 (not separately reported in HPB Foodo; trans fat presence is acknowledged in health context as likely from hydrogenated shortening but no quantified value is available from an approved source); potassium_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); calcium_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); iron_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); cholesterol_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_c_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_b6_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); folate_mcg_dfe set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_d_mcg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); vitamin_e_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); phosphorus_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); magnesium_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); zinc_mg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); selenium_mcg set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item); water_g set to -1 (not reported in HPB Foodo for this item). Sources consulted that confirmed unavailability: HPB Singapore Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) and USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 172757 (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/), both accessed 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on 'Doughnut, cake type' from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for filled donuts was found. 4. The input record included a 'plant_based' dietary tag which has been removed as filled donuts contain eggs and dairy and are not plant-based. 5. Trans fat is listed as -1 (not separately reported in HPB); however, the health context notes acknowledge the likelihood of trans fat from hydrogenated shortening used in commercial deep-frying, consistent with the original HPB record's clinical commentary.
